title: Lemonade's gross profit surges 90% as net loss narrows for 2024
text: Lemonade demonstrated significant financial improvement in 2024, with gross profit jumping 90% year-over-year to $64 million in Q4. Despite this progress, the company still faced challenges with profitability. The insurer's net loss narrowed considerably, falling from $42.4 million in Q4 2023 to $30 million in Q4 2024. This improvement reflected better underwriting discipline, as the gross loss ratio dropped substantially from 77% to 63% during the same period. Operational efficiency also improved markedly. The company's gross profit margin reached 43%, while the adjusted gross profit margin climbed to 44% from 31% the prior year. Lemonade more than doubled its growth investments, increasing spending from $55 million in 2023 to $122 million in 2024. The company maintained an LTV/CAC ratio above 3:1 and a triple-digit CAC internal rate of return (IRR) through AI-driven customer acquisition. In Force Premium reached $944 million by year-end, representing 26% growth, while total revenue hit $526.5 million for the full year. The January 2025 California wildfires impacted results, generating approximately $45 million in gross losses and a $20 million hit to adjusted EBITDA.
